title_en: Both Sam’s Club Plus and Club Memberships Have Received Huge Discounts Until July 5, Act Fast To Save on Your First Year
summary_en: Both the Plus and Club Sam’s Club memberships have been discounted to $50 for the first year and $15 for the first year, respectively. This offer only lasts until July 5.
body_en:
In the spirit of summertime savings, Sam's Club has dropped excellent offers on both its Plus and Club memberships . Until July 5, the Plus membership has dropped to just $50 for the first year, from $120, while the Club membership has plummeted to just $15 for the first year instead of $60.
Compared to their full yearly prices, this means you save over 50% on the Plus membership or 75% on the Club membership, which are some fantastic discounts to take advantage of. Only new members are allowed to join, though, but if that's you, there's quite a lot you'll get to enjoy no matter the membership option you go with.
Sam's Club is full of some great pick-ups, especially with a new membership in hand. Even now, we've spotted some exciting members-only offers on items like a Nintendo Switch 2 console , which is $449 but also comes with $15 Sam's Cash, or LEGO sets like this adorable LEGO Pokémon Eevee set , which is down 41% to $35. It's even a great spot to grab Pokemon TCG at MSRP, which is a big win for fans.
Starting with the cheaper pick of the two memberships, a Club membership sets you up with a wide variety of bonuses like limited-time discounts, member only fuel prices, Scan & Go, free curbside pickup, the Sam’s Club Mastercard, and bonus offers. You'll also get two membership cards to use, which is nice for families.
If you want even more, the Plus membership comes with all of that alongside 2% Sam's Cash back, free shipping on orders over $50, free delivery on orders over $50, early shopping hours, pharmacy and optical savings, and tire and battery center savings. For just $50 for your first year, that's an incredible amount of savings to have at your fingertips.
Hannah Hoolihan is a freelancer who writes with the guides and commerce teams here at IGN.
